hero.name: Benjamin
hero.tagline: Where Engineering Meets Innovation. bridging the gap between manufacturing processes and cutting-edge technology to eliminate waste and unlock efficiency.

bio.paragraph1: I am a passionate OPEX Engineer and Software Developer dedicated to transforming complex challenges into streamlined, automated solutions.
bio.paragraph2: With a unique blend of industrial engineering expertise and full-stack development skills, I bridge the gap between physical processes and digital efficiency. My mission is simple: eliminate waste, optimize performance, and unlock the true potential of your business.

skills.title: My Toolkit
skills.subtitle: Technologies I use to bring ideas to life

certifications.title: 18+ Professional Certifications
certifications.subtitle: Constantly upgrading my skills to stay ahead of the curve.

beyondCode.title: Beyond the Code
beyondCode.subtitle: The experiences that shaped who I am

toronto.title: Six Months in Toronto
toronto.body: At 20, I spent six months living and working in Toronto, Canada — fully immersed in English, sharing a home with people from Korea, China, India, and Canada. The multicultural environment accelerated my English fluency and broadened my worldview. I have returned for two-month stays every summer since.

legendarios.title: Legendarios Track
legendarios.body: In 2024, I completed the Legendarios Track in Tampa, Florida — five days in the wilderness with minimal food, long hikes, and heavy physical challenges. Each obstacle was a deliberate lesson in resilience and self-discovery. The experience proved that real growth lives outside the comfort zone.

books.title: Lifelong Learning
books.body: My mindset is shaped by books on proactive leadership, empathy-driven communication, financial discipline, and continuous improvement. I treat reading as a core professional habit, not leisure. The ideas I absorb directly influence how I approach engineering problems and client relationships.

basketball.title: Where I Recharge
basketball.body: When I am not coding or optimizing processes, I am on the basketball court. The sport teaches teamwork, decision-making under pressure, and the value of consistent practice — principles I carry into every engineering project. It is how I stay physically active and mentally sharp.
